Hey, Jason.
Hey, Bailey.
What's going on?
Not much.
How are you doing?
Doing pretty good.
Is it still snowy and cold and all that stuff there?
Yeah, we've had a really cold winter.
It feels like the olden days.
The olden days.
Like how we're messaging it to our kids like when we were young.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
Yeah, I think your area is just sucking up all the cold air because on the West Coast, it's pretty mild.
It's like sunny and 50, 55, something like that here.
Yeah, no, we keep getting the, you know, when your phone blares the emergency and like extreme cold warning, extreme cold, like take cover or you'll have 30 seconds.
Funny.
You know, I was going to tell you that I went and did some research after we talked and checked out the recommendation for the backpack along the river and my God.
It looks so beautiful.
the hoe?
Yeah.
Yeah, it's really cool.
That's a good one.
I'd like to go back there, maybe take my son there.
It's been a long time.
It was probably 25 years ago when I was there.
Yeah, I was showing my kids' pictures.
They were excited.

That was funny because I went with a buddy in college, and we were there three nights, I think, and we went up and camped one night up in the mountains and then came back down into the parking lot at the hoe range station.
And, you know, the whole time we were hearing these elk, the whole time, there's like, I think they're Roosevelt elk.
It's like a special kind of elk.
Yeah, you could hear them all around us, like, and we just never saw any.

And then we literally get back to the parking lot, and we're taking our packs off and, like, putting them in the car, and this giant male elk with this huge rack of, it just literally walks up right next to us, like, by the car.
And it was like, holy crap, there's this giant elk, like, in the parking lot, which is kind of a...
Funny place after being in the woods for three weeks or three days.
that's how it happened.
We went to Yellowstone this summer and we got up at like four o'clock in the morning and did this like 10-hour hike looking for like wolves.
Didn't see anything.
Get back in the car, get ready to pull away from the trail and do wolves.
Wow.
Walk out of the woods and cross the road like a foot in front of our car.
And then just like walk off into the hills.
Well, that's cool.
You got to see them, maybe not in the way you planned, but that's pretty rare to see wolves, right?
Yeah, it was.
It was sad.
We got very lucky.
It was pretty wild.
Cool.
I've not seen those before.
Hi, Michael.
Hello.
Hi.
Should I?
there we go.
Hello.
Good to see you.
Hi.
Yeah, I hit unmute and then I started talking and realized I was still on mute.
I hear you well now.
I was just using Jason as an actual travel agent.
was which.
Which feels appropriate in the subject matter.
Where are you off to?
I'm actually talking about taking our kids to Olympic National Park this summer.
So I was getting some backpacking recommendations.
Excellent.

Well, now that we're all here, I will jump in and share less of an agenda and more just some sort of guiding questions as we kind of dive in and hear more about, where you're focused and we may not have answers to everything yet.
And then you may have other things you want to talk about.
But just so you know, January reporting, I'm going to send that async later today.
But I did jump into the looker and take a quick look and it's all still trending in the right direction.
No big concerns there.
And I'll also check on the indexing again to make sure that's all happening in the way we want it to.
So in terms of today.
The goal of this is just to kind of like take a step back, take the opportunity to hear more from you about, you know, what are your business priorities?
How are we thinking about success?

Because I think we're sort of transitioning into a new stage of, you know, our content where we've been really focused on, you know, traffic and like getting people and building that indexing and like rising through those keywords.

So making sure we're aligned in terms of what our goals are, I think going forward, and then everything moves so quickly, making sure that we are still, our strategy still kind of matches your strategy.
So just talk through some of these, I know the pilot program you mentioned, and then take away everything that we talked about today.

And we'll go take a look at the strategy as it stands now, and then maybe recommend some shifts, or some new areas to expand to, or, you know, thinking about conversion, if that's where your eyes are, kind of how we could.
And move maybe more towards focusing on bottom of funnel content.
So I think there's a lot of places we can go.
So the goal is to make this a conversation so we can learn and then take it away and recommend how we might change our strategy to match your goals.

So jumping in, and I don't know if you want to talk through the pilot program first, but just getting a general sense of, you know, what are your top priorities for this year and kind of how you're thinking about measuring them would be a great place to start, I think.
And just for Michael, I think, Bailey, this is maybe a couple weeks ago, I had mentioned to you that we were starting to do a pilot with a small group of 20 people.
And so, Michael, I had just brought that up, that that could be a potential audience or target that we want to kind of create more content for.
You know, I know we've we're also talking about adding capabilities for each.
Like that could be a potential group that we want to write content for as people who manage travel for a small group of people.
Just things like that I think could be interesting.
Yeah, think there are a number of things coming up.
We are looking at introducing a price protection product where it would keep looking at the hotel and the flight you booked.
And if the price goes down, then we'd automatically switch you into that new flight and credit you the difference in the money.
We are looking at having kind of a special login delegate access for an executive assistant to be helping out a couple of different execs.
In that case, then the exec could go in and do the booking or the EA could go in and do it for them.
So there's that angle.
And yeah, we're...
The other kind of a thing, bigger thing is that we are developing a Teams, Microsoft Teams, and a Slack agent so that you'd be able to communicate with Otto directly via Teams or directly via Slack.
So all of that, I think, are product improvements and are different angles to talk about how, you know, what's good to have in corporate travel and what kind of the minimum is necessary.
But then there's also the pilots, as Jason talked about.
We have right now, I think, four companies that are going to start doing pilots with us, everywhere from a company that has about 10 travelers to one that has potential for 3,000 travelers.
But, you initially just 20 or so, we're going to be using it.
So, I'm trying to think about what angle.
I'm
There would be or what posts and blog posts there would be for eilet customers.
I think it could be even just as simple as like messaging like, hey, Otto has these capabilities to, you know, manage multiple employees travel or, you know, EA function.
Just kind of writing about those things, I think, could resonate with those people.
So I guess my question there would be, are you treating the pilot like a test to see if that's even something you want to do?
Because the other thing is you don't want to change all your messaging on all your content to message that you do this now.
And then you bring people in and then that product's like not available for their use case.
So I think some of it's thinking too about like the goal of that content.
Would it be to sort of bring in more pilot prospects?
Is it sort of like that inbound?
Are you pretty like, is your, are you pretty sure that that's an area?
That you're going to expand, in which case it's starting.
Yeah, think, as you're talking, I think here's what's interesting.
So we are partnering with Direct Travel.
Direct Travel is a well-established travel management company, or TMC.
And they're very, very high-touch, and they're amazing on service, I guess, on the high-touch human service.

And the partnership they're looking for, and that we're doing with them, and this is where a few of these pilots are originating, is for their existing TMC clients who want a better experience than their employees just going to a website or even calling in.
So it's a way to make sure those employees don't have to call in, can get all the servicing and all the transactional help from auto directly.
Interesting.
And so, I don't know if there's some, a series of blog posts.

Posts that talk about how auto can work with an existing travel management company to provide a seamless experience where you can still use the existing tools and systems of the travel management company.
You can still call in, but anything that's done on those other channels is visible through auto and auto can, you know, with conversation can help you service that, can help you change things.
Or you can do the initial transaction booking within auto as well.
So you kind of get the best of both worlds.
So is your audience in that case, is it like the endpoint user or is it the travel management company that you want to bring on as a partner?
It's probably a third one, which is the travel manager of a company.
And so companies have the, have this role sometimes sits within procurement.
Some,'s Some,
Sometimes sits within finance, but it's essentially what they call a travel manager.
the travel manager's role is to help manage, in some cases, a $2 million travel spend, and in some cases, a $500 million travel spend for that company.
You've got companies like, I mean, we're not targeting them, but you've got a company like Meta or Google or Amazon where they have employees traveling around all the time.
And they're spending hundreds of million dollars on travel.

And so this person's job, and sometimes there's a couple of these folks, their job is to help set the travel policy to make sure their employees are actually using the negotiated and discounted rates that they have, things like that.
And so these travel managers are the ones who, in many cases, they don't travel themselves, but they're responsible for the overall spend within the company.
And so they would be the ones who are making the final decision on should we...
Tryout Auto on top of these, you know, their existing relationship with direct travel or some other TMC.
And so I think what we've been building for so far is just overall visibility in the corporate travel space.

We are now moving towards doing some advertising on LinkedIn and Reddit to try to get, you know, more of the unmanaged business travelers in, the folks who work for companies that are small and don't have a travel program they're supposed to use.
And so we're trying to target those individuals through this kind of direct advertising on LinkedIn, targeted advertising on LinkedIn and Reddit.
And the second angle would be to target these larger companies or smaller companies that use a direct travel or some other TMC to try to, you know, get their entire employee base to use this as well.
So, so,
It's a little bit of a hybrid.
So I think there's a series of blog posts there.
Some of them, if they, in fact, mentioned direct travel, before we post them, we would need to run by their marketing department to use their name and to talk about them.
You know, if, in fact, it was more of a joint type of blog post, and then we could even get them to, you know, retweet it or post it on LinkedIn and kind of get more things that way.
But yeah, I think there's some angles there as well.
Do you have a description of the pilot program or like a way for people to sign up or get more?
I'm just wondering if there's like a CTA for this lane of content that we'd want to drive people towards.
There's not because we've been really relying on the direct travel account managers to go out there.
Now, I have a five slide deck that I can definitely send you.
That we've given to direct travel so they can educate their account managers on what this exactly is.
It references less the pilot program and references more what the joint capabilities of auto and direct travel together would give them.
And so maybe that's something you can feed into your system and could help generate some articles.
Yeah, so this is really helpful.
And I think we could definitely figure out how to incorporate sort of this new persona into your content.

And I think at the top of my mind, too, like we should definitely change the way we're messaging around TMCs because it feels like right now in our guidelines, it's pretty restrictive, restricted to that kind of unmanaged traveler persona as the primary.
I guess my question here would be, what would the goal of this specific lane of content be?
my be, I'll see you time.

In the sense that we have, we can, you know, aim on bringing these people to your site, but if we sort of have, if we have no way to convert them and conversion is at the top of mind for you for this type of content, I'm wondering.

terms of priority.

Like we still want to focus on both personas.

Like I don't think we go rewrite anything, but I think it'd be good to review existing blog posts and make sure they don't.

Yeah, 100%.
I was actually on my list.
I think we're getting, we usually recommend refreshing at least like every six months posts.
So I think this, given this pilot program now could be a great time to start making a priority list of posts to refresh.
And I think starting with anything.

then anything that talks about TMCs would be the way to prioritize it.

And that's like, hey, if you're interested in using auto for a pilot or whatever the language is, like get in touch and talk to us about it.
Like you could, it feels like you could have a different kind of treatment there on their posts.
I think it would be great to have some way to capture interest, like even if it's just capturing an email and then.
Yeah, I think, yeah, I think the call to action is like reach out and talk to us about this really, right?
But yeah, I think it would be really good if we don't have a place for them to like sign up.
If you bring them in, find some.
Okay.

I mean, they could still certainly sign up and try it out and kick the tires.
That's fine.

They're going to need to sit down with us, right?

So I feel like, yeah, for that.

Right?

I think some of the better ones are now, but this is where, again, the call to action of having them go in and try it.
I'm not so sure that is versus.
The, you know, I think there are cost savings that they are responsible for.
There's time savings.
If it's easier for their employees to book travel and doesn't take an hour and a half and instead takes 15 minutes, they're adding value to their company.
If instead of paying the TMC $70 for a transaction, they're paying the TMC now $20 because they're doing it on online versus call-in basis, then they're saving $50 for every transaction.
And those are the types of things that these folks care about.
That's helpful positioning too.
And yeah, it's when you're talking to a persona about their pain point, which is clearly very different than, you know, the auto's main point pain point that you're solving.
So yeah, I think we'll be thoughtful on how we kind of message that lane of content to make sure we're speaking to the things they actually care about.
It's like we have to show auto's value in two different tiers, the stuff that makes their job.
I'll
And then the stuff that will make the end user's job easier or life easier.
Okay, I feel like that's great.
That is definitely enough for us to go on and do a little research and kind of come back to you with some recommendations there.

Is there anything else in terms of, you know, your top priorities coming up or, like, focused in terms of how you're measuring success that we should sort of be aware of and thinking about when it comes When to content?
No, I think it's 100% how much organic traffic can we get or assisted traffic can we get of people coming and trying out auto and then ultimately booking and doing transactions with auto.
That is the ultimate driver right now.
Yeah, okay.
Okay.
Um, and then I think the...

Other thought is just in terms of outcomes and impact so far, A, I'd be curious, we don't totally have visibility into things beyond traffic, and are you seeing, are there any other signals that you're seeing between kind of content and signups?

I know in terms of content pillars, like that travel disruption and optimization one has performed really well so far, which feels to me like a really good sign since it maps to your core value prop and like the search intent of someone who's experiencing some of that pain and looking to solve it.
So just any thoughts you have on performance so far and how well it's mapping to your core values and any other signals you're seeing that we might not have visibility into would be great.
Yeah, I think overall, I mean, I think the traffic is the key thing and it's good to see that, you know, continue.
Continue to increase.

I'm not seeing a lot of signups necessarily coming from the blog post, but I think that's just something we can continue to, you know, grow into and hopefully it's driving signups, but I don't think that's, you know, that's not everything.
The top of the funnel stuff is good as well.
I do think I'm starting to feel like, you know, I read these several times a week.
I'm starting to feel like we're, we're kind of rehashing the same content a bit.

And I'm eager to get into, you know, some of the things that we mentioned at the beginning of the call, like that, I think there's a lot we could write about in terms of price tracking and travel credits.
think that's really exciting for people, particularly for business travel to get something back that they can use for themselves that has value.
Like there's all kinds of cool things we could write about that.
So that's just one example, like getting into new areas of content, I think we'll feel fresher.
And, and so I'm excited.
Do you have a date, like a product roadmap for when those features.
Are going to launch?
We do.
can share that.
I don't have it off the top of my head.
Michael, do you know when we're ready to do the PR around the price monitoring?
I don't.
It's not days away.
It's weeks away.
Okay.
Well, it's still even weeks.
I feel like it's the time for us to do that keyword research and spin up some article topics on that area to start.
I didn't want to do it if it was six months down the road, right?
We don't want to build a bunch of content when the feature doesn't exist yet.
But I think if you're talking in weeks, then it's definitely worth bringing that in and doing some more keyword research around those features now.
Yeah.
But yes, definitely agree.
I feel like that seems like, I think mapping more content.
I mean, your features exist because they solve an existing problem, right?
So I think being able to take that problem and connect it to a feature that solves it is a great lane of content and exactly what we'd want to expand into.
I also think, you know, thinking about...
about...
The funnel, we've seen, I know we've done some kind of comparison articles and a bunch of like lists and roundups.
We're seeing a lot of movement in these kind of articles when it comes to AI visibility.
They're far and away the thing that is being cited the most, like across all content.
And I think there's a couple of reasons.

One is that because of the way, you know, AI-assisted search like serves those chunks of content that where you get like the little paragraph, they really like content that like has pre-existing chunks.
makes it really easy.

So I think that's another area where we could kind of continue to prioritize because we're seeing and have an outsized impact on AI visibility, which, again, feels like it's a focus for you all right now.
So that might be another one to see if there are untapped opportunities there that we could lean into.
Yeah, sounds good.
Yeah, that change you made to the structured FAQ at the end.
It is great.
Yes, yeah, yeah, and that's great, again, it's another area where we're seeing a lift with that FAQ, so we'll continue to keep an eye on that.
See, I think we talked about most of this.
The open question is, like, conversion tracking.
If that's something you set up, like, definitely keep us in the loop.
I think there's a lot we can see, even with just kind of regular data exports.
We don't even necessarily have to be plugged into your system, but that would be great to kind of keep an eye on as well.

Yeah, and I confirmed with Matt, who has set up our Google Tag Manager stuff, and we have instrumentation, you know, all the way through there, so we should be able to run reports and get you any of that kind of information you need.
and you guys have access to our, you have access to our Google Analytics, right?
We do, we have, yeah.
So our looker pulls directly.
So we have Google Search Console and GA4.
Okay, so I think we're cool on all the conversion tracking stuff.
Awesome.
And then one other quick note, I'm about to send messages on Slack, but we did today just launch the AI visibility tool that we developed that I've been talking to you about.
So it just launched as of about two hours ago.
So I was planning to record just a quick Loom demo walkthrough, and I'll send that this week in case you want to take a look.

And then I think hopefully next week we'll be getting clients, starting to get clients login access so you can actually go in, like see those prompts yourself, like poke around, see how you're performing, see what else is being cited.
So that feels also like a really good way for you to go in and get visibility and for us to kind of talk through how we might want to adjust things like prompts.
Good.
Good.
ready.
And the new expansion into the new persona.
Cool.
Okay, I think that was all the things I wanted to talk about.
I know we're almost out of time, but can you scroll down?
Number five, second bullet, new content performance.
It just kind of occurred to me, and I'm interested to hear your thoughts.
Often the way that Otto shows up in this content is there's a topic and there's several paragraphs, and then we kind of weave in like, hey, there's a product called Otto the Agent.
Here's the link.
And it does X, Y, and Z kind of in sentence format.
Should we have some posts that go deeper into really like how Otto works with some screenshots?
Or like, we don't really do any of that.
We have a few videos.
We could do more.
I just wonder like, yeah, basically.
Yeah, I think definitely.
I think, A, anytime you can show visuals or even better video, like anything interactive, there's definitely like a huge.
Potential to do that.
And so I think we'd have to think about which types of content we'd want to do that.
But I think, yeah, absolutely.
If that's something that you're interested in exploring, that would be great to see inside the product.
I think it could be helpful.
It definitely has more of a salesy feel, right?
It's like we're really promoting and diving deep into auto, but that's what we do.

It feels okay if that's kind of the I mean, I think especially if you're connecting it back to that idea of pain and problems and solutions and features, I think especially if you're doing it in the context of a, like, you have some sort of pain or problem, and, like, we have a feature that provides a solution and sort of, like, a here's how it works.
I think it, I mean, you always have to balance, depending on the type of content, education, and, like, putting your brand forward.
But I do think that there are places you can do it in a way that's not necessarily going to, you know, damage it from an organic search perspective.
Yeah, Bailey, thank you.
I have to.
Unfortunately, this has been awesome.
Thank you.
Thank you so much.
And then the only last thing I would say before you jump is that also the other thing we talked about at some point was the CTAs.
Yeah.
Like those kind of sticky CTAs or like more embedded CTAs where you have an opportunity to show a visual and like invite someone kind of into the product in a way that's reusable.
So you can like map, you use this CTA when you're talking about this pain point and then you can kind of just automate the way it gets pulled in.
Yeah, That's another really kind of low touch and easy way to do it because you could just develop a library of them and then decide when you want to pull in which one.
Totally.
So you're not trying to get in there and like record videos and, you know, screenshot your product.
They already exist.
Yeah.
No, that makes perfect sense.
Yeah.
Okay.
I can think about that.
I know you'd shared some examples too.
I could imagine us doing a small one in the almost like carving.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align content strategy with Otto's 2026 business priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Persona:** Add "Travel Managers" as a secondary persona to support the Direct Travel TMC partnership. This content will focus on validation, not direct conversion, to build credibility.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=542.0)
  - [**Content Refresh:** Prioritize refreshing existing content to align with the new TMC partnership and integrate upcoming features like price protection.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1031.0)
  - [**Deeper Product Content:** Create more detailed, feature-focused content with screenshots and video to better demonstrate Otto's value.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1692.0)
  - [**New CTA Strategy:** Test reusable, visual CTAs (e.g., sticky right-column widgets) to increase product engagement and track performance.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)

## Topics

### 2026 Business Priorities

  - [**Pilot Program:** A 20-person pilot is launching with four companies (10–3,000 travelers) to test new features.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=451.0)
  - [**Product Roadmap:** Upcoming features create new content opportunities:](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
      - [**Price Protection:** Automatically rebooks flights/hotels at lower prices.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
      - [**Delegate Access:** Enables EAs to manage travel for multiple executives.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
      - [**Teams/Slack Agents:** Allows direct interaction with Otto via chat apps.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)

### New Persona: Travel Managers

  - [**Goal:** Support the Direct Travel TMC partnership by targeting Travel Managers.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=542.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This content provides validation for TMC account managers presenting Otto to their clients.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=914.0)
      - [**Why:** Prospects research Otto post-pitch (via Google, ChatGPT), and existing content builds trust and confirms Otto is a "real thing."](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=914.0)
  - [**Content Focus:** Educate on TMC-specific pain points and Otto's value, using their language.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1155.0)
      - [**Key Metrics:** Cost savings (e.g., $70 call-in vs. $20 online transaction) and time savings.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
  - [**CTA:** A "get in touch" form, not a self-serve signup, to capture qualified leads for the sales team.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1127.0)

### Content Strategy & Performance

  - [**Primary Goal:** Drive organic and assisted traffic leading to user signups and transactions.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1276.0)
  - [**Performance:**](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
      - [Traffic is trending up.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=217.0)
      - [The "travel disruption and optimization" pillar performs well, aligning with core value prop.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1341.0)
      - [Comparison/list articles are highly cited by AI search, boosting visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)
  - [**Challenge:** Content feels repetitive; new features offer a chance to create fresh, exciting topics.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1398.0)

### New Content Formats & CTAs

  - [**Deeper Product Demos:** Create content showing "how Otto works" with screenshots and video.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1692.0)
      - [**Why:** Better demonstrates value and moves beyond high-level mentions.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1692.0)
      - [**Framing:** Connect features directly to solving user pain points.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1762.0)
  - [**Reusable CTAs:** Test reusable, visual CTAs (e.g., sticky right-column widgets).](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
      - [**Why:** Low-effort way to increase product engagement and track performance.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
      - [**Test:** Start with 3 CTAs to measure their effectiveness.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1855.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
      - [Send January report and check site indexing.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=217.0)
      - [Research Travel Manager persona and keywords to propose a new content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
      - [Prioritize content refresh, starting with TMC-related posts and top-ranking articles.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1031.0)
      - [Send Loom demo of the new AI visibility tool; provide client login access next week.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1632.0)
  - [**Otto:**](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=814.0)
      - [Share the 5-slide deck for the Direct Travel partnership to inform content creation.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=814.0)
      - [Provide the product roadmap for upcoming features (e.g., price protection).](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1434.0)
      - [Implement and test 3 reusable CTAs to measure engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/tUf33mP35_hJytxuSotzuJLyNVaJzP7M?tab=summary&timestamp=1855.0)
